channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: 5 ways AI is helping spur the EV era
item.description: From innovations like autopilot systems to battery management optimization, the electric vehicle (EV) industry has consistently leveraged and improved cutting-edge technologies to enhance vehicle performance and user experience.
item.dc:creator: Yaron Saghiv, Fast Company

item.title: Revolutionizing Data Analytics with AI-Powered GPUs
item.description: Discover how Voltron Data's innovative software harnesses the power of GPUs to efficiently handle massive data analytics tasks and transform businesses.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Agentic AI: Why this emerging technology will revolutionise multiple sectors
item.description: New advancements in GenAI technology are set to create more transformative opportunities for tech-savvy enterprises and organisations. These developments come as data shows that while the GenAI boom is real and optimism is high, not every organisation is generating tangible value so far.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: What to expect from AI in the enterprise in 2025
item.description: This year saw the initial hype and excitement over AI settle down with more realistic expectations taking hold.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The Double-Edged Sword of Generative AI in Software Development: Balancing Productivity and Maintainability
item.description: Generative AI has revolutionized software development, but its benefits are not equally distributed among developers. As experienced engineers accelerate their implementation, maintainable code requires constant supervision and correction.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Surveying the LLM application framework landscape
item.description: Large language models by themselves are less than meets the eye; the moniker “stochastic parrots” isn’t wrong.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Salesforce Expands into Dubai: A Strategic Move for MENA Region Growth
item.description: Salesforce opens new office in Dubai, UAE, to support local businesses and drive digital transformation across the MENA region.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Father and son, business development reps a generation apart
item.description: Starting out in sales as a business development rep (BDR) is like boot camp for the business world. You learn resilience, understand rejection, and develop grit—qualities that stick with you throughout your career.
item.dc:creator: Jason Zintak, Fast Company

item.title: ACLU spurns 'flawed and dangerous' ruling on TikTok ban
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union (ACLU) condemned Friday’s “flawed and dangerous” federal appeals court ruling that upheld the law banning the social media platform TikTok.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: The AI-powered grandma taking on scammers
item.description: Daisy is an artificial intelligence-powered grandmother created to interact with scammers to waste their time and gather information about their tactics.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: IAEA chief: Iran is poised to 'quite dramatically' increase stockpile of near weapons-grade uranium
item.description: MANAMA, Bahrain (AP) — Iran is poised to “quite dramatically” increase its stockpile of near weapons-grade uranium as it has started cascades of advanced centrifuges, the head of the International Atomic Energy Agency warned Friday.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: CEO of UnitedHealthcare Killed: Unpacking the Tensions Roiling American Life
item.description: The killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson has sparked a national debate on health insurance, medical debt, and social media reactions.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, NewsNation

item.title: Federal appeals court upholds law requiring sale or ban of TikTok in the US
item.description: A federal appeals court panel on Friday unanimously upheld a law that could lead to a ban on TikTok as soon as next month, handing a resounding defeat to the popular social media platform as it fights for its survival in the U.S. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Two senators look to reign in big tech’s influence in defense AI, cloud contracts
item.description: The bill would require the Defense Department to a competitive award process when contracting with cloud, advanced AI systems, or data infrastructure vendors. The post Two senators look to reign in big tech’s influence in defense AI, cloud contracts first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: OpenAI releases o1 LLM, unveils ChatGPT Pro
item.description: OpenAI has officially released its OpenAI o1 large language model (LLM), built for complex reasoning. The company also introduced ChatGPT Pro, a $200 monthly plan that includes unlimited access to OpenAI o1, o1-mini, GPT-4o, and advanced voice conversations.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: After shooting, UnitedHealthcare comes under scrutiny for AI use in treatment approval
item.description: In the wake of the murder of its CEO this week, UnitedHealthcare has come under greater scrutiny for its use of an allegedly flawed AI algorithm that overrides doctors to deny elderly patients critical heathcare coverage.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: You.com founder Richard Socher’s plan to win the AI search wars
item.description: In 2020, Richard Socher left Salesforce to build the next big search engine. Google was king back then, but he felt its flagship product was woefully inefficient.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Musk spent at least $250M to help elect Trump, filings show
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk spent at least $250 million to help propel President-elect Trump back into the White House, the latest reports filed with the Federal Election Commission (FEC) show.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Apple’s M4 Mac mini is a tiny miracle. Inside the drastic redesign
item.description: I can’t overstate how radical Apple’s Mac mini was when it debuted in January 2005. It was tiny, measuring just 6.5 inches squared and two inches high—the smallest Mac ever.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Better together? Why AWS is unifying data analytics and AI services in SageMaker
item.description: Data warehousing, business intelligence, data analytics, and AI services are all coming together under one roof at Amazon Web Services.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Ultimate guide to ChatGPT, Gemini, Llama, and other genAI chatbots you need right now
item.description: Since OpenAI first released ChatGPT to the public in November 2022, there has been a proliferation of consumer-facing chatbots in the large language model (LLM) race, changing how consumers interact with information. Chatbots are the interface we use to interact with LLMs.
item.dc:creator: Janya Sundar, Fast Company

item.title: Character.AI is under fire for hosting pro-anorexia chatbots
item.description: “Hello, I am here to make you skinny,” opens the conversation on popular startup Character.AI. “Remember, it won’t be easy, and I won’t accept excuses or failure,” the bot continues.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: China's ban on key high-tech materials could have broad impact on industries, economy
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has banned exports of key materials used to make a wide range of products, including smartphones, electric vehicles, radar systems and CT scanners, swiping back at Washington after it expanded export controls to include dozens of Chinese companies that make equipment used to pro
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Michigan Democrats move to protect reproductive health data before GOP takes control of House
item.description: Democrats in Michigan are pressing to pass reproductive health care legislation before the party loses its majority with the new legislative session next year.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Volmert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 8 phishing email scams to watch out for this holiday season
item.description: Be on alert this holiday season for phishing emails. Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson shares several examples of fake phishing emails he's received in his inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Founder of failed crypto lending platform Celsius Network pleads guilty to fraud charges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The founder and former CEO of the failed cryptocurrency lending platform Celsius Network could face decades in prison after pleading guilty Tuesday to federal fraud charges, admitting that he misled customers about the business.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Agencies still struggling to meet digital accessibility standards
item.description: GSA has said that limited staffing and resources are key drivers of agencies’ ongoing challenges with digital accessibility and Section 508 conformance. The post Agencies still struggling to meet digital accessibility standards first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Drew Friedman, Federal News Network

item.title: What to make of Pat Gelsinger’s abrupt exit
item.description: The retirement of Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger has left more questions than Intel answered in its statement regarding his departure. Intel in the past had a measured process of ascension for future CEOs.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: China bans exports to US of gallium, germanium, antimony in response to chip sanctions
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China announced Tuesday it is banning exports to the United States of gallium, germanium, antimony and other key high-tech materials with potential military applications, as a general principle, lashing back at U.S. limits on semiconductor-related exports.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, NewsNation
